data.title: BlackRock deposits $181 million worth of ETH and BTC into Coinbase.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, BlackRock deposited 68,500 ETH (worth $139 million) and 612 BTC (worth $41.4 million) into Coinbase.

data.title: Centralized exchanges still offer stronger liquidity, with Gate.com maintaining its position among the top three in perpetual contract market share.
data.description: According to data disclosed by The DeFi Report analyst @JustDeauIt on social media, in the past 30 days, Binance ranked first in the global perpetual contract market with a market share of 39.23%, OKX accounted for 17.96%, and Gate maintained its leading position globally with a market share of 11.93%, continuing to solidify its leading position in the derivatives trading field. He pointed out that although market attention to decentralized perpetual trading platforms continues to rise, in terms of actual trading data, centralized trading platforms still possess a stronger user base and liquidity capacity in the current market environment. This trend has, to some extent, corrected market expectations of "decentralized trading dominating the market." As the market enters a period of volatility, perpetual contracts are becoming a core tool for capital speculation and strategy execution. Gate, with its continuously increasing market share and trading depth, further strengthens its competitive advantage in the global derivatives market and provides users with a more stable and efficient trading experience.

data.title: Analysis: The US and Iran may be entering a phase of fighting while negotiating.
data.description: Iran has formally responded to the 15-point ceasefire agreement proposed by the United States. Does this signify a softening of Iran's stance, or does it indicate that Iran seeks to gain more leverage in the negotiations? Furthermore, while advancing the negotiation process, the United States may continue to increase its troop deployment in the Middle East. What does this "fighting while negotiating" trend signify? Will the risk of conflict in the Middle East continue to escalate? Qin Tian, ​​deputy director of the Middle East Institute of the China Institutes of Contemporary International Relations, analyzes that in the short term, neither the US nor Iran wants to rashly escalate the war, but at the same time, they are unable to reach a mutually beneficial outcome through negotiations. Both sides intend to postpone resolving the difficult issue of how to end the war, first buying themselves time for reflection and decision-making. However, while the risk of escalation in the US-Israel-Iran conflict is being delayed, it is also increasing. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Bitget CEO: US stock market crash accelerates macroeconomic risk reassessment, Bitcoin shows resilience
data.description: In response to today's sharp decline in US stocks, which wiped out over $1 trillion in market capitalization in a single day, Bitget CEO Gracy Chen pointed out that this round of adjustment reflects that global markets are reassessing macroeconomic risks at a faster pace. As rising oil prices further fuel inflation concerns, the impact of geopolitical changes is no longer limited to the energy market but is beginning to more directly affect global capital allocation. Gracy believes that against this backdrop, Bitcoin will remain highly volatile in the short term, but compared to previous periods of sharp declines in risk appetite, Bitcoin's performance this time has been relatively stable. This reflects a significant decrease in the overall leverage ratio of the crypto market, thus limiting the scale of forced liquidations that typically amplify downward pressure during periods of market stress. This relative resilience sends a more noteworthy signal: in an increasingly fragmented macroeconomic environment, Bitcoin is beginning to be viewed as a more neutral allocation option by some investment portfolios.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks generally fell in pre-market trading in the US, with COIN down 2.31%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, crypto stocks generally fell in pre-market trading in the US, with COIN down 2.31%, MSTR down 1.06%, SBET down 0.46%, and BMNR down 0.15%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Ripple CTO Refutes Claims of Pre-Allocated XRP Contracts
data.description: David Schwartz, Ripple's Chief Technology Officer, has denied allegations circulating on social media that he confirmed the existence of secret pre-allocated XRP contracts for certain investors. According to NS3.AI, the rumor suggested that a significant portion of Ripple's escrowed XRP was already designated for undisclosed recipients. This has reignited discussions about misinformation prevalent in XRP-focused social media circles.

data.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Uncertainty Over Cease-Fire Prospects
data.description: Oil prices have seen an upward trend as skepticism grows regarding the potential for a cease-fire in ongoing geopolitical conflicts. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the initial optimism surrounding a possible resolution has waned, leading to a decline in stock market recovery. The uncertainty has prompted investors to reassess their positions, with energy markets reacting to the fluctuating sentiment. Analysts suggest that the lack of concrete progress in peace talks is contributing to the volatility in both oil prices and stock markets. As the situation develops, market participants remain cautious, closely monitoring any developments that could impact global economic stability.

data.title: A whale unstaking 170,000 SOL tokens and deposited them into Kraken, leaving $38.9 million worth of SOL still staked.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, in the past 24 hours, a whale unstaking 170,000 SOL tokens, worth $14.85 million, and deposited them into Kraken. Currently, this whale still has 457,937 SOL tokens staked, worth $38.9 million.

data.title: Baupost Group's Q4 2025 Moves: New Amazon Stake and Reduced Alphabet Holdings
data.description: Baupost Group has revealed a significant investment in Amazon, amounting to $489.659 million, during the fourth quarter of 2025. According to NS3.AI, the investment firm also acquired shares in Molina Healthcare and Grupo Aeromexico, as detailed in its latest 13F filing. Additionally, Baupost Group reduced its stake in Alphabet Class A by 41% and decreased its holding in CRH by 68%.

data.title: A new address withdrew 2,650 BTC from Binance, worth $179.6 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, a new address withdrew 2,650 BTC from Binance today, worth $179.6 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Holdings Increase Among Large Wallets as Exchange Reserves Decline
data.description: Bitcoin wallets containing between 10 and 10,000 BTC have collectively increased their holdings by 61,568 BTC over the past month, marking a 0.45% rise. According to NS3.AI, this accumulation comes as Bitcoin reserves on centralized exchanges have dropped to approximately 2.7 million BTC, reaching their lowest level since 2019.

data.title: Saudi Arabia's Yanbu Exports Surge, Avoiding Hormuz Strait
data.description: Saudi Arabia's Yanbu port is experiencing a significant increase in export activity, as the country seeks to bypass the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that this move comes amid rising geopolitical tensions in the region, prompting Saudi Arabia to explore alternative routes for its oil exports. The Yanbu port, located on the Red Sea, offers a strategic advantage by providing a direct route to international markets without passing through the volatile Hormuz Strait. This development is part of Saudi Arabia's broader strategy to ensure the security and reliability of its oil exports, which are crucial to the global energy market. The increased activity at Yanbu highlights the kingdom's efforts to diversify its export routes and reduce dependency on the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments. As geopolitical uncertainties persist, Saudi Arabia's focus on alternative export pathways underscores its commitment to maintaining stable energy supplies to the world.

data.title: Blue Owl's $1.4 Billion Asset Sale Fails to Boost Market Confidence
data.description: Weeks after Blue Owl announced a $1.4 billion asset sale, market confidence in its private-credit empire remains tepid. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that some previously undisclosed details about the structure of the deal might shed light on the market's cautious stance. Despite the significant size of the transaction, investors appear wary, possibly due to concerns about the underlying assets or the terms of the sale. The lack of enthusiasm highlights ongoing skepticism in the private-credit sector, which has faced scrutiny over transparency and risk management. As Blue Owl navigates these challenges, the market's response underscores the importance of clear communication and robust deal structures in maintaining investor trust.

data.title: UXLINK Announces Strategic Upgrade with AI Social Network
data.description:
On March 27, Web3 social infrastructure project UXLINK announced a strategic upgrade to its ecosystem, introducing the UXLINK Agent & AI Social Network. According to BlockBeats, UXLINK plans to leverage its extensive Social Growth Layer by integrating AI Agent technology, unveiling a new UX Agent roadmap. This Agent is compatible with major global models, enabling individuals and project teams to optimize content, automate generation, and perform intelligent searches on social media. The goal is to enhance social account growth and value capture, empowering millions of users to benefit from the Web3 ecosystem.

Additionally, UXLINK will strategically upgrade its business model and tokenomics. The new Agent requires integration with AI Tokens to meet computational resource needs, prompting the team to initiate a community vote based on transparency and fairness to align the existing token model with the AI narrative.

UXLINK emphasizes that the core objective of this upgrade is to build an intelligent social network where humans and Agents coexist. The team assures that the interests of current holders will be protected throughout the upgrade process, ensuring community members share in the long-term benefits of the AI social era.

data.title: Crypto Market Structure Bill Sparks Industry Debate; Traditional Exchanges Develop Tokenized Products
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter) that the recent compromise on the crypto market structure bill has led to divisions within the industry. The bill aims to establish a regulatory framework for digital assets, but differing opinions have emerged regarding its potential impact.

Meanwhile, three major traditional exchanges are actively developing tokenized products, signaling a shift towards integrating blockchain technology into conventional financial systems. This move is seen as a response to the growing demand for digital asset solutions.

This week, the total market value of RWA (Real World Assets) on the blockchain has shown steady growth. The number of asset holders is increasing at a faster rate than the overall market size, indicating a rising interest in tokenized assets.

Stablecoin market capitalization remains stable, consolidating within the platform. However, there has been a continuous decline in monthly active addresses and transaction volumes, suggesting a potential slowdown in market activity.

data.title: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard announced that the Strait of Hormuz is closed and any passage through the waterway will face "severe measures."
data.description: According to Iranian media, the Iranian Revolutionary Guard stated that the Strait of Hormuz has been closed, and any passage through the waterway will face "severe measures." All vessels "traveling to or from ports of hostile allies and supporters of the US and Israel" are prohibited from passing through any waterway or heading to any destination. (Reuters)

data.title: The yield on French 10-year government bonds rose to its highest level since June 2009.
data.description: The yield on French 10-year government bonds rose to its highest level since June 2009, reaching 3.876%, up 8 basis points from the previous day. (Jinshi)

data.title: Backpack founder: The company has not monetized through OTC channels and will re-examine the Sybil attack case.
data.description: Backpack founder Armani Ferrante stated on the X platform that, regarding recent rumors about Backpack, the company has not cashed out through OTC token sales. Previous posts on Discord were merely to assist buyers in finding tokens. Regarding Mad Lads, all users who held Mad Lads before the TGE will retain their Backpack VIP status, while new holders will not, a move designed to align incentives with long-term holders. In addressing Sybil attacks, the goal is to protect retail investors from unfair competition from professional players, but acknowledges that previous vetting processes were too absolute and will continue to review Sybil attack cases. Furthermore, he believes that short-term price and FDV after the TGE are not core metrics for success; the team's incentive mechanisms are deeply tied to token performance, and they are committed to creating long-term value.

data.title: Bank of Japan's Neutral Interest Rate Estimates Suggest Potential for Further Rate Hikes
data.description: The Bank of Japan has released new estimates indicating that Japan's neutral interest rate is between 1.1% and 2.5%, which is higher than the current policy rate of 0.75%. According to NS3.AI, the report highlights that several indicators in the latest estimates suggest a gradual increase in interest rates. The bank noted that this could potentially raise the upper limit of the neutral range, providing more scope for additional rate hikes.
